question: As it relates to sex education, Aliya's father advised that

option.a: she should abstain totally from sex as this is the worst sin ever in the history of human civilisation
option.b: she should wisely decide before letting go of her body
option.c: she should be mindful of her company of friends and the media's influence on sexual affairs
option.d: sexual affair is no longer a hidden issue and she should wisely make her choices

section: Questions below are based on Bolaji Abdullahi's "Sweet Sixteen"
solution: Mr Bello warned Aliya to be careful as regarding the kind of movies, books and progammes she entertain as well the company of friends she keeps.

question: She was so depressed that she just wanted to eat. ....3.... by a neighbour, the mobile care unit from Nsambya hospital went to her home.
section:
Choose the most appropriate option for each group for questions

Alice, a small fragile-looking Ugandan mother, is dying of AIDS. She lives with her son and daughter in a tin-roofed shack on the .....1... of Kampala. When her husband died of AIDS in 1987, Alice discovered she was HIV positive. Frequent ...2..... of sickness forced her to give up her embroidery job. She was so depressed that she just wanted to eat. ....3.... by a neighbour, the mobile care unit from Nsambya hospital went to her home. They convinced her to start taking proper ..4....... After three months of ....5.... treatment, she regained her will to live. The AIDS time bomb .....6... on relentlessly. The World Health Organization ....7.... that 1.8 million Ugandans are HIV positive, nearly one in eight of the total population. The Ugandan AIDS Commission set up by President Museveni ..8...... the spread of the disease. However, under-porting and the lack of post-mortem and ...9..... makes it impossible to establish exact figures. No one ....10.... that it is a disaster far worse than the years of civil war.
solution: A neighbor who saw and observed Alice decided to come to her aid by alerting the mobile care unit

question: Both questions are alternatives; you answer ........ one ........ the other
solution: Option A is the best answer. Note that 'either' goes with 'or' and the usage of both creates an impression as to alternatives.

question: <u>Procrastination</u> is the enemy of progress
section: Choose the one opposite in meaning to the word(s) underlined.
solution: When someone doesn't procrastinate or postpone things unnecessarily for example out of laziness, then he or she accelerates the pace of the work required.

question: Some men will continue to cause offences until they are given [a taste of their own medicine]
section: Choose the one nearest in meaning to the word(s) in bracket
solution: Recompensed for is the correct answer. To have 'a taste of your own medicine' is to have the same unpleasant experience that one has caused another.

question: Hadn't it rained, it wouldn't have been so bad

option.a: It was so bad because it didn't rain
option.b: it rained and so it wasn't bad
option.c: it wasn't bad because it rained
option.d: it rained and that made it so bad

solution: The usage of the anomalous verb 'had' alongside with the negatives; 'not' and that in 'wouldn't' means that, it actually rained and that didn't make things good.

question:
Choose the word that has the same rhyme scheme.

Dice
solution: The sound is obtainable in the pronunciation of 'dice' and 'rice'.
